channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Tools & Frameworks
channel.description: Latest Tools & Frameworks news in Context Engineering

item.title: Uncertainty-Aware Sequential Decision Rules for Event-Triggered LLM Invocation in Streaming Systems
item.description: arXiv:2607.13048v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Streaming inference pipelines increasingly pair lightweight fast models with Large Language Models (LLMs) that provide rich semantic understanding at substantial cost. The central question of when to invoke the LLM has received limited formal treatmen...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, framework, arxiv, LLM, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: awesome-prompt-engineering - A curated list of resources, tools, papers, and platforms for prompt engineering in large language models (LLMs) and generative AI.
item.description: A curated list of resources, tools, papers, and platforms for prompt engineering in large language models (LLMs) and generative AI.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, model, LLM, platform, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: SPINE: Bridging the Cyber-Physical Gap with Agentic AI
item.description: arXiv:2607.13049v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Foundation models have given robots a sophisticated brain for complex decision-making, yet deploying that intelligence into a physical platform still demands tedious, expert-driven calibration. This deployment gap, the robot's spinal cord, remains a p...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, framework, arxiv, context, platform | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Federated Explainable Artificial Intelligence: Roles, Architectures, Evaluation, and Open Challenges
item.description: arXiv:2607.13045v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Federated Learning (FL) has emerged as a key paradigm for privacy-preserving collaborative model training across distributed and heterogeneous data sources. By keeping raw data local, FL addresses data confidentiality concerns, yet it does not resolve...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, framework, arxiv, tool, attention | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: fastapi_mcp - Expose your FastAPI endpoints as Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools, with Auth!
item.description: Expose your FastAPI endpoints as Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools, with Auth!<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, API, tool, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: code-review-graph - Local-first code intelligence graph for MCP and CLI. Builds a persistent map of your codebase so AI coding tools read only what matters, with benchmarked context reductions on reviews and large-repo workflows.
item.description: Local-first code intelligence graph for MCP and CLI. Builds a persistent map of your codebase so AI coding tools read only what matters, with benchmarked context reductions on reviews and large-repo workflows.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Graph-Based Detection of Disinformation Narrative Diffusion between Russian and Ukrainian Telegram Channels
item.description: arXiv:2607.11894v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Detecting disinformation narratives on social media is challenging due to the scale of amplification, rapid evolution, and linguistic variability of online content. We propose a graph-based framework for identifying and analyzing disinformation narrat...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> vision, model, framework, API, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Self-Improvements in Modern Agentic Systems: A Survey
item.description: arXiv:2607.13104v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Self-improving autonomous agents are moving from research prototypes to deployed systems. The primary goal is controllable evolution, or adaptation, from experience with minimal or even no human input. This survey frames modern self-improving agents a...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> research, memory, model, framework, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
